name: Taj Mahal
title: An Immortal Symbol of Love
description: Majestically located on the banks of the Yamuna River, the Taj Mahal is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the Seven Wonders of the World, built by Emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his beloved wife Mumtaz Mahal.

name: Red Fort
title: The Crown of Mughal Architecture
description: The magnificent red sandstone fortress that served as the main residence of Mughal emperors for nearly 200 years. The site where India's Prime Minister hoists the national flag on Independence Day.

name: Golden Temple
title: The Spiritual Heart of Sikhism
description: Harmandir Sahib, the Golden Temple, is the holiest Gurdwara of Sikhism, shimmering in 24-karat gold. Its sacred pool and free community kitchen (langar) serving thousands daily make it one of India's most humbling sites.

name: Amer Fort
title: A Hilltop Marvel of Rajput Glory
description: Perched on a rugged hillside overlooking Maota Lake, Amer Fort blends Hindu and Mughal architecture with breathtaking mirrored halls, royal chambers, and panoramic views of the Pink City.

name: Hawa Mahal
title: The Iconic Palace of Winds
description: The five-storey pink sandstone façade with 953 intricately carved windows is Jaipur's most photographed landmark. Built in 1799, it allowed royal ladies to observe street festivities while remaining unseen.

name: Humayun's Tomb
title: The Precursor to the Taj Mahal
description: Built in 1570, this UNESCO World Heritage Site was the first garden-tomb in the Indian subcontinent, inspiring the design of the Taj Mahal. Set in a Persian-style char bagh, its symmetry is awe-inspiring.

name: Qutub Minar
title: The World's Tallest Brick Minaret
description: A soaring 73-metre minaret of intricate red sandstone and marble built in 1193, Qutub Minar is a UNESCO World Heritage Site surrounded by ruins of ancient mosques and the famous iron pillar that has resisted rusting for 1,600 years.

name: India Gate
title: A Tribute to Fallen Heroes
description: The 42-metre tall war memorial arch commemorates the 70,000 soldiers of the British Indian Army who died in World War I. The eternal flame of Amar Jawan Jyoti burns beneath its arch, honoring the nation's brave soldiers.

name: Gateway of India
title: Mumbai's Iconic Welcome Arch
description: Overlooking the Arabian Sea, the Gateway of India was built in 1924 to commemorate the visit of King George V. Today it is Mumbai's most iconic landmark, from where boats depart to the Elephanta Caves.

name: Mehrangarh Fort
title: The Mighty Fort of the Blue City
description: Rising 410 feet above Jodhpur, Mehrangarh Fort is one of the largest forts in India. Its elaborate palaces, intricate latticework windows, and sweeping views of the Blue City make it a traveller's delight.

name: Mysore Palace
title: A Royal Legacy Illuminated in Light
description: One of the most visited monuments in India, the Indo-Saracenic Mysore Palace glows with 97,000 light bulbs every Sunday. The opulent halls, golden howdah, and jewels of the Wadiyar dynasty are legendary.

name: Lotus Temple
title: A Flower-Shaped Sanctuary of Peace
description: Shaped like an unfolding lotus with 27 marble petals, the Bahá'í House of Worship welcomes people of all faiths. It is one of the most visited buildings in the world, set within serene gardens.

name: Ellora Caves
title: Rock-Cut Temples of Three Faiths
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, Ellora's 34 cave temples carved between the 6th and 11th centuries represent Buddhist, Hindu, and Jain traditions. The Kailasa Temple (Cave 16), carved from a single rock, is the world's largest monolithic structure.

name: Dal Lake
title: The Jewel of Kashmir
description: Surrounded by the snow-capped Himalayas, Dal Lake is famous for its iconic shikaras (wooden boats), floating markets, and the luxurious houseboats offering unique accommodation on the shimmering water.

name: Statue of Unity
title: The World's Tallest Statue
description: Standing at 182 metres, the Statue of Unity is a tribute to Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, the Iron Man of India. The statue, twice the height of the Statue of Liberty, offers a viewing gallery at 153 metres with spectacular views.

name: Agra Fort
title: The Mightiest Mughal Fortress
description: This UNESCO-listed fortress served as the main residence of Mughal emperors until 1638. Shah Jahan, who built the Taj Mahal, spent his last years imprisoned here, gazing at his wife's mausoleum from the Musamman Burj tower.

name: Golconda Fort
title: The Diamond Capital of the Medieval World
description: Once the source of legendary diamonds including the Koh-i-Noor, Golconda Fort is renowned for its brilliant acoustics where a hand clap at the entrance can be heard atop the fort, a mile away. The sound and light show is unmissable.

name: Chittorgarh Fort
title: The Pride of Rajput Valor
description: The largest fort in India, Chittorgarh is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a symbol of Rajput chivalry and sacrifice. Its towers, palaces, and temples tell the tales of Rani Padmini and epic acts of jauhar (self-immolation).

name: Fatehpur Sikri
title: Akbar's Abandoned Mughal Capital
description: Built by Emperor Akbar in 1571 as his imperial capital, this UNESCO World Heritage Site was abandoned after just 14 years due to water scarcity. The Buland Darwaza, at 54 metres, remains the largest gateway in the world.

name: Jantar Mantar
title: The Ancient Observatory of the Stars
description: Built by Maharaja Jai Singh II in 1734, this UNESCO-listed astronomical observatory features 19 major geometric instruments for measuring time, predicting eclipses, and tracking celestial bodies with remarkable precision.

name: Meenakshi Temple
title: A South Indian Temple of Wonders
description: The Meenakshi Amman Temple is a historic Hindu temple with 14 gopurams (gateway towers) covered in thousands of multi-coloured sculptures. The temple is a masterpiece of Dravidian architecture and one of the oldest in India.

name: Vaishno Devi
title: The Sacred Mountain Shrine
description: Set 5,200 feet above sea level in the Trikuta Mountains, the Vaishno Devi shrine is one of the most important Hindu pilgrimage sites, attracting over 8 million devotees annually for the arduous but spiritually rewarding trek.

name: Sun Temple
title: A 13th-Century Chariot of the Sun God
description: Built in the 13th century in the shape of a colossal chariot with 24 elaborately carved stone wheels pulled by seven horses, the Konark Sun Temple is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a triumph of Orissan architecture.

name: Kashi Vishwanath Temple
title: The Holiest Shiva Temple in India
description: One of the twelve Jyotirlinga shrines of Shiva, the Kashi Vishwanath Temple on the banks of the Ganges has been a centre of Hindu faith for thousands of years. The newly expanded temple corridor offers a breathtaking view of the sacred ghats.

name: Akshardham Temple
title: A Modern Marvel of Devotion
description: Built in just five years using 6,000 tonnes of pink sandstone, Akshardham is a modern temple complex featuring intricate carvings, a musical fountain, and an IMAX-style cultural show that brings ancient Indian history to life.

name: Jama Masjid
title: India's Largest Mosque
description: Built by Shah Jahan between 1644 and 1656, Jama Masjid can accommodate 25,000 worshippers in its expansive courtyard. Its three gateways, four watchtowers, and twin 40-metre minarets dominate the skyline of Old Delhi.

name: Bangla Sahib Gurudwara
title: Delhi's Most Prominent Sikh Shrine
description: Associated with the eighth Sikh Guru, Har Krishan, this magnificent gurudwara with its golden dome and sacred sarovar (pond) offers free langar to hundreds daily. Its calm atmosphere and selfless service inspire all visitors.

name: Ajanta Caves
title: Masterpieces of Buddhist Rock Art
description: Carved between the 2nd century BCE and 480 CE, the 30 rock-cut Buddhist cave monuments at Ajanta feature stunning paintings and sculptures depicting the life of the Buddha. A UNESCO World Heritage Site of global significance.

name: Elephanta Caves
title: A Trident of Stone Carvings
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site on a small island in Mumbai Harbour, the Elephanta Caves house magnificent rock-cut temples dedicated to Shiva. The 6-metre-tall Maheshamurti, depicting Shiva with three faces, is among India's greatest sculptures.

name: Victoria Memorial
title: Kolkata's Colonial White Marble Gem
description: Built between 1906 and 1921 to commemorate Queen Victoria's 25-year reign, this grand white marble building now serves as a museum. Set amid sprawling gardens, it is Kolkata's most iconic and photographed monument.

name: Charminar
title: The Heart of the City of Pearls
description: Built in 1591 by Muhammad Quli Qutb Shah to mark the end of a plague, the Charminar (Four Towers) is the emblem of Hyderabad. Surrounded by a bustling bazaar selling pearls, bangles, and biryani, it throbs with life.

name: Howrah Bridge
title: An Engineering Marvel Over the Hooghly
description: The cantilever Howrah Bridge, completed in 1943, carries over 100,000 vehicles daily across the Hooghly River — with no nuts or bolts, held together entirely by rivets. The bridge lights up gloriously at night.

name: Marine Drive
title: Mumbai's Queen's Necklace at Night
description: The 3.6 km C-shaped boulevard along the Arabian Sea is lined with Art Deco buildings and glittering streetlights that resemble a diamond necklace at night. The promenade is the city's favourite escape at sunset.

name: Kerala Backwaters
title: Cruising Through God's Own Country
description: An intricate network of lagoons, lakes, canals, and rivers stretching parallel to the Arabian Sea coast. A houseboat cruise through the backwaters — past paddy fields, coconut groves, and quaint villages — is an unforgettable experience.

name: Munnar Hill Station
title: Tea Gardens in the Misty Hills
description: Perched at 1,600 metres in the Western Ghats, Munnar is a breathtaking landscape of rolling tea estates, mist-clad mountains, and cool climate. The Eravikulam National Park nearby is home to the endangered Nilgiri Tahr.

name: Valley of Flowers
title: A Himalayan Carpet of Wildflowers
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site in the Zanskar Range, the Valley of Flowers bursts into a riot of colour during the monsoon with over 300 species of wildflowers. Accessible only by trekking, it is a paradise for botanists and nature lovers.

name: Rann of Kutch
title: The World's Largest Salt Desert
description: The vast white salt marshes of the Rann of Kutch stretch to the horizon, turning silver under a full moon during the annual Rann Utsav festival. The flamingo sanctuary and local Kutchi crafts add to the experience.

name: Andaman Islands
title: Pristine Beaches of the Indian Ocean
description: The Andaman Islands offer some of the world's finest beaches and coral reefs. From the powdery white sands of Radhanagar Beach (ranked among Asia's best) to scuba diving with manta rays, the islands are India's tropical paradise.

name: Goa
title: India's Beach Capital of Laid-Back Life
description: With 105 km of palm-fringed coastline, Portuguese colonial churches, spice plantations, and a vibrant nightlife, Goa is India's most popular tourist destination. From Baga's beach shacks to Palolem's secluded coves, there's a Goa for everyone.

name: Lakshadweep Islands
title: India's Coral Island Treasure
description: India's smallest union territory, Lakshadweep is a group of 36 coral atolls with turquoise lagoons and pristine white beaches. With extremely limited tourism, the islands offer untouched natural beauty and exceptional snorkelling.

name: Jim Corbett National Park
title: India's First and Oldest National Park
description: Established in 1936, Jim Corbett is India's oldest national park and a premier wildlife destination. Home to over 200 Bengal tigers, 500 elephant species, and 600 bird species, a jungle safari here is an unforgettable wildlife experience.

name: Ranthambore National Park
title: Tiger Country Meets Ancient Ruins
description: Ranthambore is famous for its tigers — the apex predators here are so bold they are often spotted even in daylight. The dramatic backdrop of the 10th-century Ranthambore Fort rising above the jungle makes every sighting more spectacular.

name: Kaziranga National Park
title: Home of the One-Horned Rhinoceros
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, Kaziranga holds two-thirds of the world's great one-horned rhinoceroses and has the highest density of tigers in any wildlife reserve. Elephant safaris through tall grasslands offer extraordinary wildlife encounters.

name: Sundarbans
title: The World's Largest Mangrove Delta
description: The Sundarbans is the world's largest river delta and home to the legendary Royal Bengal Tiger — the only tiger in the world adapted to living in saltwater mangroves and swimming between islands. A UNESCO World Heritage Site of extraordinary ecology.

name: Brihadeeswara Temple
title: A 1000-Year-Old Granite Masterpiece
description: Built by Raja Raja Chola I in 1010 CE, the Brihadeeswara Temple is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of India's greatest achievements of architecture. The 66-metre vimana (tower) casts no shadow at noon on the ground.

name: Hampi
title: The Ruined City of the Vijayanagara Empire
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site spread across surreal boulder-strewn landscapes, Hampi was once one of the richest cities in the world as the capital of the Vijayanagara Empire. The magnificent Virupaksha Temple and stone chariot are iconic.

name: Mahabalipuram
title: Shore Temples on the Bay of Bengal
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, Mahabalipuram features extraordinary 7th-century rock-cut temples, cave sanctuaries, and open-air bas-reliefs carved directly from granite boulders. The Shore Temple overlooking the sea is breathtaking.

name: Khajuraho Temples
title: Poetry in Stone of the Chandela Dynasty
description: Built between 950 and 1050 CE, the Khajuraho group of monuments is a UNESCO World Heritage Site renowned for their nagara-style architectural symbolism and erotic sculptures. The Western Group of temples is especially magnificent.

name: Varanasi Ghats
title: The Spiritual Soul of India
description: The 84 ghats (stepped riverbanks) of Varanasi along the Ganges are among the world's oldest living urban landscapes. The nightly Ganga Aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat — with fire, chants, and thousands of lamps — is a profoundly moving spectacle.

name: Haridwar & Rishikesh
title: The Gateway to the Himalayas
description: Haridwar, where the Ganges descends from the Himalayas to the plains, is one of India's holiest cities. Nearby Rishikesh — the Yoga Capital of the World — offers ashrams, whitewater rafting, bungee jumping, and the famous suspension bridge Laxman Jhula.

name: Pangong Lake
title: The High-Altitude Lake That Changes Colour
description: At 4,350 metres in the Himalayas, Pangong Lake shimmers in shades of blue, green, and purple throughout the day. Extending 134 km across the India-China border, it is the world's highest saltwater lake and made famous by the film 3 Idiots.

name: Nubra Valley
title: Where Sand Dunes Meet Snow Peaks
description: Accessible via the world's highest motorable road (Khardung La at 5,602m), Nubra Valley offers the surreal sight of Bactrian (double-humped) camels on sand dunes surrounded by snow-capped Himalayan peaks.

name: Coorg
title: The Scotland of India
description: Known as Kodagu, Coorg is India's premier coffee-producing region draped in lush rainforests, misty valleys, and cascading waterfalls. The Kodava people, renowned warriors with a unique culture, call this paradise home.

name: Jal Mahal
title: The Floating Palace of Jaipur
description: The five-storey royal palace appears to float in the middle of Man Sagar Lake. With only the top floor visible above the waterline, it creates a magical mirage-like reflection. The lake is also a bird sanctuary for migratory flamingoes.

name: City Palace
title: The Royal Residence of Jaipur
description: The City Palace is a majestic complex of palaces, gardens, and courtyards that serves as the official residence of the royal family of Jaipur. The Chandra Mahal within the complex offers the most stunning views of the Pink City.

name: Dargah Sharif Ajmer
title: A Sufi Shrine of Universal Harmony
description: The Dargah of Khwaja Moinuddin Chishti is one of the most important Sufi shrines in South Asia, visited by millions of devotees of all faiths annually. The qawwali (devotional music) performances create an atmosphere of profound spiritual energy.

name: Somnath Temple
title: The First of the Twelve Jyotirlingas
description: One of the most sacred Hindu temples in India, Somnath was looted and destroyed multiple times throughout history but has been rebuilt each time. Today's magnificent temple stands on the Gujarat coast with its spire reflecting on the Arabian Sea.

name: Haji Ali Dargah
title: A Sacred Island Shrine in the Arabian Sea
description: Located on a tiny islet off the coast of Worli, the Haji Ali Dargah is accessible only at low tide via a narrow causeway. This Sufi shrine of the Muslim saint Haji Ali Shah Bukhari attracts devotees of all faiths and offers magical views of Mumbai.

name: Sarnath
title: Where the Buddha First Taught
description: The deer park of Sarnath is where Gautama Buddha gave his first sermon after attaining enlightenment. The Dhamek Stupa, built in 500 CE, marks the spot. The Sarnath Museum houses the iconic Lion Capital of Ashoka — India's national emblem.
